pattern: Process towers from the largest cap to the smallest, greedily taking one less than the previous assigned height whenever the cap allows -- that keeps every assignment as large as possible while guaranteeing distinctness.
hints: Sort the caps from largest to smallest -- the tower with the biggest cap should get first pick of the tallest available height.
Track the height assigned to the previous tower in that order; the current tower can be at most (previous - 1), and never more than its own cap.
If the best height you can still legally give a tower drops below 1, no valid assignment exists -- return -1 immediately.

edge_inputs.description: minimum caps everywhere, still just barely assignable
two towers share the same minimal cap, impossible to make distinct
duplicate caps that are still assignable via the greedy squeeze
single tower at the maximum cap
many towers tied at the same large cap

stress.description: large roster of towers with caps skewed toward small values to stress infeasibility and the squeeze together
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    maximumHeight = [rng.randint(1, max(2, n // 5)) for _ in range(n)]
    return [maximumHeight]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maximumTotalSum(self, maximumHeight):
        n = len(maximumHeight)
        order = sorted(range(n), key=lambda i: maximumHeight[i])
        best = [-1]

        def backtrack(i, used, total):
            if i == n:
                if total > best[0]:
                    best[0] = total
                return
            cap = maximumHeight[order[i]]
            # with only n towers total, an optimal assignment never needs a
            # candidate more than n below the cap -- any lower value could
            # always be swapped for one of the top (n+1) without creating a
            # new collision, so bounding the search here is exhaustive in
            # effect while keeping this brute force independent of cap size.
            lo = max(0, cap - n)
            for h in range(cap, lo, -1):
                if h not in used:
                    used.add(h)
                    backtrack(i + 1, used, total + h)
                    used.remove(h)

        backtrack(0, set(), 0)
        return best[0]
